prerequisites: An active Appfigures account with API access.

An API Client created in the Appfigures developer portal at https://appfigures.com/developers/keys, configured with the scopes you need (scopes are inherited by the token and cannot be changed later).
A Personal Access Token generated from that API Client (this is the value used for the connector's api_key field).
A start_date value marking the earliest date from which to sync data.

authMethods.name: Personal Access Token (API Key)
authMethods.summary: The connector's api_key secret is an Appfigures Personal Access Token (PAT). Appfigures recommends PATs for internal ETLs, dashboards, and scripts that access only your own account's data. The token is sent as a Bearer token in the Authorization header (Authorization: Bearer <token>). A PAT inherits the scopes of the API Client it is created under, and the token value is shown only once at creation.

setupSteps.title: Create an API Client in Appfigures
setupSteps.body: Log in to your Appfigures account and go to https://appfigures.com/developers/keys (My API Keys). Create an API Client, which provides the client_key/secret_key and defines the scopes. Choose scopes carefully, because you cannot modify the scopes of an existing client afterward and the Personal Access Token inherits them.

setupSteps.title: Generate a Personal Access Token
setupSteps.body: Open the details of the API Client you want to use and choose Create Personal Access Token. Copy the token immediately and store it securely, since Appfigures displays it only once and there is no way to view it again after it is issued.

setupSteps.title: Provide the token as api_key
setupSteps.body: Enter the Personal Access Token as the connector's required api_key field. The connector authenticates by sending it as a Bearer token in the Authorization header.

setupSteps.title: Set the start date and optional fields
setupSteps.body: Set the required start_date to the earliest date you want to sync data from. Optionally set search_store, the store to search in streams (default Apple), and group_by, the category term used to group search results (default product).
